programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programme.desc: Beneath the earth, and hidden in the depths of the sea, lie some of the deadliest discoveries of all time. From an asteroid impact that nearly ended life on Earth to creatures once thought to be myths.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: The world is full of strange and mind-bending places, but some take the cake. Explore an island ruled by deadly predators or a mysterious paranormal hotspot that's a magnet for UFOs and strange sightings.

programme.title: The Mega-Brands That Built The World
programme.desc: When Motorola introduces the first handheld portable telephone to the world, the futuristic technology sets off a fierce battle among telecommunications companies to produce the most popular cell phone.

programme.title: The Pickers
programme.desc: In Texas, Retro Rick opens the doors to his stockpile of Hollywood relics, and Robbie is determined to reunite two halves of an ultra-rare gas globe.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: For thousands of years, people have believed that everyday objects can possess hidden energy. Talismans are seen as providing protection and good fortune, and some are thought to embody a higher power.

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programme.desc: Beneath the earth, and hidden in the depths of the sea, lie some of the deadliest discoveries of all time. From an asteroid impact that nearly ended life on Earth to creatures once thought to be myths.

programme.title: The Mega-Brands That Built The World
programme.desc: When Motorola introduces the first handheld portable telephone to the world, the futuristic technology sets off a fierce battle among telecommunications companies to produce the most popular cell phone.

programme.title: History's Greatest Escapes With Morgan Freeman
programme.desc: An Iranian American psychopath is thrown into city jail in Southern California. Before he gets sent to a higher security prison, he escapes by crawling up the plumbing chase of the five-story prison.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: One of the darkest chapters in early American history. In 1692, fear rips through the town of Salem. Accusations fly, and many people are put to death. But what really sparked the Salem Witch Trials.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: We've heard the tales of prison breaks, but these aren't your average escapes. From a man who frees himself all thanks to a fruit to a Soviet pilot that evades capture by stealing the enemy's plane.

programme.title: Holy Marvels With Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: In the Bible, there are two Arks, each designed by God himself. Both the Ark of the Covenant and Noah's Ark were built to protect but lost to history. Did either exist, and if so, where could they be.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A set of replica Muppets has Chum feeling green with envy; but will going gonzo over these land him the goods? Meanwhile, Rick flips over an autograph book from the 1800s, but are the signatures the real.

programme.title: The Pickers
programme.desc: The competitive pinball-wizard Wolfe brothers meet coin-op king Dirk at his giant poultry barn turned private arcade. Mike and Robbie enter a hangar full of ultra-rare games and choice carnival relics.

programme.title: The Toys That Built The World
programme.desc: The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles high-kick their way into the action figure market in the late 1980s, opening the door for a new kind of martial arts toy. But their place at the top is soon challenged.

programme.title: The Food That Built The World
programme.desc: Two entrepreneurs rise from the ashes of Prohibition by staking everything on two liquors and go on to save an industry, change cocktail culture, and create two of the most popular brands in the country.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: The sushi episode delves into the story of one of Japan's most popular dishes. The episode examines the history of sushi, from its humble beginning as a method of preserving fish.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: The episode examines the history of tempura from Nagasaki tempura, the prototype of today's tempura derived from fritters brought to Japan by 16th-century Portuguese/Spanish traders and missionaries.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A set of replica Muppets has Chum feeling green with envy; but will going gonzo over these land him the goods? Meanwhile, Rick flips over an autograph book from the 1800s, but are the signatures the real.

programme.title: The Toys That Built The World
programme.desc: The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles high-kick their way into the action figure market in the late 1980s, opening the door for a new kind of martial arts toy. But their place at the top is soon challenged.

programme.title: The Food That Built The World
programme.desc: Two entrepreneurs rise from the ashes of Prohibition by staking everything on two liquors and go on to save an industry, change cocktail culture, and create two of the most popular brands in the country.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: We've heard the tales of prison breaks, but these aren't your average escapes. From a man who frees himself all thanks to a fruit to a Soviet pilot that evades capture by stealing the enemy's plane.

programme.title: Holy Marvels With Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: In the Bible, there are two Arks, each designed by God himself. Both the Ark of the Covenant and Noah's Ark were built to protect but lost to history. Did either exist, and if so, where could they be.

programme.title: The Pickers
programme.desc: The competitive pinball-wizard Wolfe brothers meet coin-op king Dirk at his giant poultry barn turned private arcade. Mike and Robbie enter a hangar full of ultra-rare games and choice carnival relics.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: The sushi episode delves into the story of one of Japan's most popular dishes. The episode examines the history of sushi, from its humble beginning as a method of preserving fish.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: The episode examines the history of tempura from Nagasaki tempura, the prototype of today's tempura derived from fritters brought to Japan by 16th-century Portuguese/Spanish traders and missionaries.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: We've heard the tales of prison breaks, but these aren't your average escapes. From a man who frees himself all thanks to a fruit to a Soviet pilot that evades capture by stealing the enemy's plane.

programme.title: Holy Marvels With Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: In the Bible, there are two Arks, each designed by God himself. Both the Ark of the Covenant and Noah's Ark were built to protect but lost to history. Did either exist, and if so, where could they be.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A set of replica Muppets has Chum feeling green with envy; but will going gonzo over these land him the goods? Meanwhile, Rick flips over an autograph book from the 1800s, but are the signatures the real.

programme.title: The Pickers
programme.desc: The competitive pinball-wizard Wolfe brothers meet coin-op king Dirk at his giant poultry barn turned private arcade. Mike and Robbie enter a hangar full of ultra-rare games and choice carnival relics.

programme.title: The Toys That Built The World
programme.desc: The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles high-kick their way into the action figure market in the late 1980s, opening the door for a new kind of martial arts toy. But their place at the top is soon challenged.

programme.title: The Food That Built The World
programme.desc: Two entrepreneurs rise from the ashes of Prohibition by staking everything on two liquors and go on to save an industry, change cocktail culture, and create two of the most popular brands in the country.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: The sushi episode delves into the story of one of Japan's most popular dishes. The episode examines the history of sushi, from its humble beginning as a method of preserving fish.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: The episode examines the history of tempura from Nagasaki tempura, the prototype of today's tempura derived from fritters brought to Japan by 16th-century Portuguese/Spanish traders and missionaries.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: We've heard the tales of prison breaks, but these aren't your average escapes. From a man who frees himself all thanks to a fruit to a Soviet pilot that evades capture by stealing the enemy's plane.

programme.title: The Pickers
programme.desc: The competitive pinball-wizard Wolfe brothers meet coin-op king Dirk at his giant poultry barn turned private arcade. Mike and Robbie enter a hangar full of ultra-rare games and choice carnival relics.

programme.title: The Food That Built The World
programme.desc: Two entrepreneurs rise from the ashes of Prohibition by staking everything on two liquors and go on to save an industry, change cocktail culture, and create two of the most popular brands in the country.

programme.title: Holy Marvels With Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: In the Bible, there are two Arks, each designed by God himself. Both the Ark of the Covenant and Noah's Ark were built to protect but lost to history. Did either exist, and if so, where could they be.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A set of replica Muppets has Chum feeling green with envy; but will going gonzo over these land him the goods? Meanwhile, Rick flips over an autograph book from the 1800s, but are the signatures the real.

programme.title: History's Greatest Escapes With Morgan Freeman
programme.desc: During the Civil War, a daring prison break frees hundreds of Union soldiers. When he was captured and sent to Libby Prison in Richmond, Virginia, he engineered one of the greatest mass escapes in history.

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programme.desc: Beneath the earth, and hidden in the depths of the sea, lie some of the deadliest discoveries of all time. From an asteroid impact that nearly ended life on Earth to creatures once thought to be myths.

programme.title: Lost U-Boats Of Wwii
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Darrell Miklos and his team make a discovery far older than they expected. Later, new evidence emerges suggesting that the team may have uncovered a long-lost battle at Target 4.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: In July 1996, one of the worst aviation disasters in American history leaves 230 people dead and countless questions unanswered. It's a tragedy that changes the aviation industry forever.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: The history books say Adolf Hitler committed suicide in the final days of World War II, but not everyone believes there's sufficient proof. Did the Nazi leader die in Berlin, or was he able to escape.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Puma Punku in Bolivia features some of the largest stone blocks on Earth, each carved with incredible precision. Could new technology solve an old puzzle-and offer proof of alien intervention on Earth.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: The clock is ticking with only days remaining in python season. Pickle seeks validation from Troy on her tracking skills. Bruce must hunt alone in Zak's absence.

programme.title: Lost U-Boats Of Wwii
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Darrell Miklos and his team make a discovery far older than they expected. Later, new evidence emerges suggesting that the team may have uncovered a long-lost battle at Target 4.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: The history books say Adolf Hitler committed suicide in the final days of World War II, but not everyone believes there's sufficient proof. Did the Nazi leader die in Berlin, or was he able to escape.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Puma Punku in Bolivia features some of the largest stone blocks on Earth, each carved with incredible precision. Could new technology solve an old puzzle-and offer proof of alien intervention on Earth.

programme.title: History's Greatest Escapes With Morgan Freeman
programme.desc: During the Civil War, a daring prison break frees hundreds of Union soldiers. When he was captured and sent to Libby Prison in Richmond, Virginia, he engineered one of the greatest mass escapes in history.

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programme.desc: Beneath the earth, and hidden in the depths of the sea, lie some of the deadliest discoveries of all time. From an asteroid impact that nearly ended life on Earth to creatures once thought to be myths.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: In July 1996, one of the worst aviation disasters in American history leaves 230 people dead and countless questions unanswered. It's a tragedy that changes the aviation industry forever.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: The clock is ticking with only days remaining in python season. Pickle seeks validation from Troy on her tracking skills. Bruce must hunt alone in Zak's absence.

programme.title: History's Greatest Escapes With Morgan Freeman
programme.desc: During the Civil War, a daring prison break frees hundreds of Union soldiers. When he was captured and sent to Libby Prison in Richmond, Virginia, he engineered one of the greatest mass escapes in history.

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programme.desc: Beneath the earth, and hidden in the depths of the sea, lie some of the deadliest discoveries of all time. From an asteroid impact that nearly ended life on Earth to creatures once thought to be myths.

programme.title: Lost U-Boats Of Wwii
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Darrell Miklos and his team make a discovery far older than they expected. Later, new evidence emerges suggesting that the team may have uncovered a long-lost battle at Target 4.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: In July 1996, one of the worst aviation disasters in American history leaves 230 people dead and countless questions unanswered. It's a tragedy that changes the aviation industry forever.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: It's the unsolved air piracy case in history. A man hijacks a Northwest Orient Airlines flight, demanding 4 parachutes and $200,000. He jumps off the plane with the money and is never seen again.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Puma Punku in Bolivia features some of the largest stone blocks on Earth, each carved with incredible precision. Could new technology solve an old puzzle-and offer proof of alien intervention on Earth.
